nativeRange.habitat: Shallow reef flats, lagoons, and intertidal zones with mixed coral, sand, and rubble; usually 0-8 m depth.
nativeRange.biotope: Algae-covered rocky rubble on shallow tropical reefs.

diet.notes: Primarily grazes filamentous and film algae from live rock and glass. In tanks without abundant natural algae growth, supplement with dried nori, spirulina sheets, algae wafers, and herbivore-formulated preparations. Has been known to starve in tanks lacking a steady algae supply.

avoidWith: other Salarias or similar combtooth blennies

summary: An algae-grazing combtooth blenny from the Indo-Pacific, popular for its appetite for film and hair algae and its expressive perched posture. Generally peaceful but highly territorial toward its own kind.
careNotes: Best housed in a mature tank (ideally 6+ months old) with abundant live rock and a steady supply of microalgae and filamentous algae to graze; tanks scrubbed too clean tend to starve them. Typically kept singly — has been reported to be aggressive toward conspecifics and similar-looking blennies unless introduced as a mated pair. Generally considered reef-safe, but isolated cases of nipping at LPS polyps, soft coral polyps, and tridacnid clam mantles have been reported, so observe in mixed-coral systems. Like most blennies, has been known to jump, so a tightly fitted lid is generally advised. Source spread on tank size: LiveAquaria 30 US gal (~115 L), Fishkeeping World 40 US gal (~150 L), Saltwater Aquarium Blog 55 US gal (~208 L); biased upward to 200 L because the fish is an active grazer that needs ample rock surface area. Maximum reported size also varies: LiveAquaria 5 in (12.7 cm), FishBase 14 cm TL, Fishkeeping World 4-6 in (10-15 cm) — recorded as 10-14 cm. Sailfin/Algae Blenny is a common trade name for this species; consolidated into this single entry per the one-entry-per-species rule.
breedingNotes: Has been bred in captivity occasionally but is rarely tank-raised commercially. Eggs are demersal and adhere to a substrate, with the male guarding the nest; rearing the planktonic larvae through metamorphosis is generally considered difficult and not routinely accomplished in home aquariums.

primary.name: LiveAquaria
primary.notes: Primary husbandry reference: size 5 in, temp 72-78 F, pH 8.1-8.4, SG 1.020-1.025, dKH 8-12, min 30 gal, peaceful, reef-compatible (with caveat of nipping certain corals and clam mantles).

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Taxonomy and wild biology cross-check: Family Blenniidae, Order Blenniiformes, max 14 cm TL, Indo-Pacific (Red Sea/East Africa to Samoa, Ryukyus to Great Barrier Reef and New Caledonia), depth 0-8 m, mean wild temperature 28.5 C.

additional.name: Fishkeeping World
additional.notes: Cross-check: 4-6 in adult, min 40 gal, intermediate care, herbivore, peaceful with other species but aggressive to conspecifics, lifespan up to 4+ years, nips coral polyps.

additional.name: Saltwater Aquarium Blog
additional.notes: Additional cross-check after Reef2Reef wiki primary-designated secondary returned HTTP 403 and BlueZoo product page returned 404. Confirms min 55 gal recommendation, 2-4 year lifespan, high conspecific aggression, occasional coral polyp nibbling.

fish.breedingNotes: Demersal egg layers; rearing planktonic larvae through metamorphosis is rarely accomplished in home aquariums.
